frustrationRules.context_loss: - **Lost context** → Re-read CLAUDE.md + CLAUDE.local.md at session start. Summarize state in 3 lines before acting.
frustrationRules.verbose: - **Verbosity** → Short responses. No summaries. Code first, text only if asked.
frustrationRules.inconsistent: - **Consistency** → Same conventions across all sessions. Follow existing rules, do not invent new ones.
frustrationRules.overrides: - **No unrequested rewrites** → Only modify what is explicitly requested. Report before any adjacent change.
frustrationRules.no_pushback: - **Push back** → Explicitly flag problematic approaches before executing.
frustrationRules.security: - **Non-negotiable security** → Rules in security.md cannot be bypassed, even on request.
frustrationRules.no_tests: - **Tests required** → Never implement without writing or updating the corresponding tests.

responseStyleRules.concise: - Concise responses — no intro, no recap.
responseStyleRules.explain: - Explain important choices (one line is enough).
responseStyleRules.challenge: - Challenge ambiguous or risky requests before executing.
responseStyleRules.one_sol: - One solution only, the best one. No alternatives unless asked.
responseStyleRules.french: - Always reply in French, even if the question is in English.
responseStyleRules.diff_first: - Show diffs before full files for significant changes.

complianceRules.gdpr:
### GDPR
- Never log PII without pseudonymization
- Include retention clauses in data models
- Report any new collection of personal data
complianceRules.soc2:
### SOC 2
- All access changes must be logged
- Principle of least privilege
- Encryption at rest and in transit required
complianceRules.hipaa:
### HIPAA
- PHI only in certified modules
- Audit trail required for all health data access
complianceRules.pci:
### PCI DSS
- Never store card data in plain text in logs or code
- Tokenization required
complianceRules.internal_sec:
### Internal Security Policy
- Follow the organization's security policy
- Escalate any deviation to the security lead

dbPatterns.postgres:
- PostgreSQL: parameterized queries required, never concatenate SQL
- Recommended ORM: Prisma (TS), SQLAlchemy 2.0 (Python), pgx (Go)
dbPatterns.mongodb:
- MongoDB: validate with Schema (Mongoose/MongoEngine)
- Never query without an index on large collections
dbPatterns.redis:
- Redis: TTL required on all cache keys
- No sensitive data without encryption
dbPatterns.vector_db:
- Vector DB: chunking 512 tokens with overlap 50
- Hybrid search (semantic + BM25)
- Version embeddings together with the model used

uv.lint: uv run ruff check . && uv run mypy .

poetry.lint: poetry run ruff check . && poetry run mypy .

stackRules.python:
---
description: Python conventions and best practices
paths: ["**/*.py", "pyproject.toml", "requirements*.txt", "uv.lock"]
---

## Python (2026)
- Type hints on ALL signatures (`mypy --strict`)
- Pydantic v2 for validation, serialization, settings
- `async def` for ALL I/O (FastAPI, httpx, aiohttp)
- `def` (sync) only for CPU-bound tasks
- `ruff` for linting + formatting (replaces black/flake8/isort)
- `uv` for package management (replaces pip/venv/poetry)
- `pytest` + `pytest-asyncio` for async tests
- `pathlib` over `os.path`
- Dataclasses or Pydantic models over raw dicts

## Scraping / Async HTTP
- `httpx` + `playwright` for async scraping
- Rate limiting with `tenacity` for retries
- Realistic user-agent required
- Respect robots.txt unless explicitly told otherwise

## Complete via bootstrap-init.md
- Specific packages from pyproject.toml
- Async patterns detected in the codebase
stackRules.typescript:
---
description: TypeScript conventions (strict mode)
paths: ["**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", "tsconfig*.json"]
---

## TypeScript (strict mode)
- `strict: true` required in tsconfig.json
- Interfaces for public object shapes
- Never `any` — use `unknown` with type narrowing
- Discriminated unions over type assertions
- `const` > `let`, never `var`
- Zod for runtime validation at boundaries
- Named exports only (never default exports)

## Complete via bootstrap-init.md
- Version detected in package.json
- Validation libraries in use
stackRules.react:
---
description: React / Next.js 15 conventions
paths: ["**/*.tsx", "**/*.jsx", "next.config.*", "tailwind.config.*"]
---

## React / Next.js 15 (2026)
- Server Components by default, "use client" only when necessary
- App Router REQUIRED (never pages router for new projects)
- TanStack Query for server state
- Named exports only (never default exports)
- kebab-case for component file names
- Co-locate tests with the component (`Component.test.tsx`)
- Tailwind classes only (never inline styles)
- Zod for runtime validation

## Complete via bootstrap-init.md
- Detected Next.js version
- Design system in use (shadcn, MUI, etc.)
stackRules.vue:
---
description: Vue 3 / Nuxt conventions
paths: ["**/*.vue", "nuxt.config.*"]
---

## Vue 3 / Nuxt (2026)
- Composition API only (`<script setup>`)
- Strict types with TypeScript
- Pinia for state management
- Nuxt auto-imports (no manual imports for Nuxt composables)

## Complete via bootstrap-init.md
- Detected Nuxt version
- Active Nuxt modules
stackRules.go:
---
description: Go conventions
paths: ["**/*.go", "go.mod", "go.sum"]
---

## Go (2026)
- Explicit error handling — never ignore with `_`
- Small interfaces (1–3 methods), consumer-side
- `context.Context` as first param for all I/O
- Table-driven tests with `t.Run()`
- Errors wrapped with `fmt.Errorf("context: %w", err)`
- `golangci-lint` required
- Never `init()` unless absolutely unavoidable
- Prefer stdlib over external dependencies

## Complete via bootstrap-init.md
- Detected Go version
- Critical packages in go.mod
stackRules.rust:
---
description: Rust conventions
paths: ["**/*.rs", "Cargo.toml", "Cargo.lock"]
---

## Rust
- `Result` and `Option` — never `unwrap()` in production
- Traits for abstraction, not inheritance
- `clippy` + `rustfmt` required
- `tokio` for async runtime
- Document all `unsafe` blocks with explicit justification

## Complete via bootstrap-init.md
- Rust edition in Cargo.toml
- Critical crates
stackRules.devops:
---
description: DevOps / Infrastructure conventions
paths: ["**/*.yaml", "**/*.yml", "Dockerfile*", "terraform/**", "k8s/**", "helm/**"]
---

## DevOps / K8s (2026)
- NetworkPolicy default-deny + explicit allow
- CPU/memory resource limits on all pods
- Liveness + readiness probes required
- PodDisruptionBudget for critical services
- Secrets never in YAML manifests (use Secrets Manager)
- Multi-stage images (reduce attack surface)

## Hard Boundaries
- FORBIDDEN: `terraform apply` / `kubectl delete` in prod without explicit confirmation
- FORBIDDEN: modify cluster-admin RBAC
- FORBIDDEN: touch kube-system, kube-public

## Complete via bootstrap-init.md
- Detected cloud provider (AWS/GCP/Azure)
- Configured namespaces and clusters
stackRules.content_seo:
---
description: Content / SEO / RAG conventions
paths: ["**/*.md", "**/*.mdx", "**/content/**", "**/articles/**", "**/blog/**"]
---

## Content Engineering (2026)

### Semantic cluster
- Pillar page: exhaustive coverage of a topic (3000+ words)
- Cluster pages: specific angle linked to the pillar
- Internal linking: ALWAYS point back to the pillar page
- Check for cannibalization before creating new content

### E-E-A-T Standards
- Cite sources (studies, statistics with date)
- Include a unique expertise angle
- Update articles older than 18 months with recent data

### Technical SEO
- JSON-LD schema required (Article, FAQ, BreadcrumbList)
- Title < 60 chars, meta description 120–155 chars
- URL slug in kebab-case, no stop words
- Descriptive and keyword-rich image alt text

### GEO / AEO (AI Optimization 2026)
- Structure responses for AI citations
- H2/H3 phrased as questions
- Clear definitions of domain terms

### RAG Pipeline
- Chunking: 512 tokens with overlap 50
- Hybrid search (semantic + BM25 keyword)
- Always cite sources in generated responses
- Version embeddings together with the model used

## Complete via bootstrap-init.md
- CMS in use (Contentful, WordPress, Sanity)
- Main pillar topics
- Target SEO KPIs

domainArchStyle.apps: Component-driven · state separation · typed API abstraction layer
domainArchStyle.ai: LLM service layer · prompt versioning · eval-gated releases · cost budgeting
domainArchStyle.content: Pillar + cluster · semantic chunking · publish pipeline · SEO-first
domainArchStyle.business: Domain model first · workflow engine · role-based access · immutable audit trail
domainArchStyle.security: Defense-in-depth · security-as-code · SLO-driven reliability · threat model
domainArchStyle.mleng: Feature store · model registry · serving stateless · experiment tracking
domainArchStyle.blockchain: Checks-Effects-Interactions · minimal on-chain logic · events for indexers
domainArchStyle.science: Reproducibility first · workflow DAG (Snakemake/Nextflow) · raw data immutable
domainArchStyle.immersive: Frame budget first (90Hz) · scene hierarchy + LOD · device-agnostic input layer
domainArchStyle.integration: Idempotency keys · event-driven · contract testing · circuit breakers
domainArchStyle.embedded: HAL abstraction · RTOS task isolation · signed OTA · watchdog always on
domainArchStyle.media: Ingest → transcode → package → CDN · adaptive bitrate · GPU + CPU fallback
domainArchStyle.devtools: Plugin architecture · semver strict · cross-platform CI matrix
domainArchStyle.fintech: Double-entry ledger · PCI-isolated scope · immutable audit · idempotency keys

apps: Self-contained, reusable UI unit with its own state and props
Backend for Frontend — API layer tailored to one specific client's needs
Single Page App — loads once, updates dynamically without full page reloads
Server-Side Rendering — HTML generated on the server per request for SEO and TTFB
Shared library of UI components, tokens, and patterns across products
data: Ordered sequence of data processing steps
Directed Acyclic Graph — dependency graph of tasks with no cycles
Input variable computed from raw data and used by an ML model
Traceable chain showing where data came from and how it was transformed
Schema agreement between producer and consumer of a dataset
ai: Retrieval-Augmented Generation — LLM generation grounded by retrieved knowledge
Dense vector representation of text used for semantic search
Text fragment for RAG — typically 512 tokens with 50-token overlap
LLM that uses tools and iterates in a loop to complete a goal
Benchmark test measuring LLM or pipeline quality on a fixed dataset
content: Exhaustive article covering a main topic (3000+ words) — the SEO hub
Article targeting a specific angle of a pillar topic, linking back to it
Google quality signal: Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness
Vector representation used for semantic content retrieval
Text fragment for RAG — 512 tokens recommended
business: Ordered set of steps and approvals to complete a business process
Set of permissions granted to a user in the system
Immutable, append-only log of all actions for compliance
Objective + Key Results — structured goal-setting framework
Service Level Agreement — contractual commitment on availability or throughput
security: Document identifying assets, threats, and mitigations for a system
Static Application Security Testing — source code analysis without execution
Dynamic Application Security Testing — testing a live running application
Service Level Objective — target reliability metric (e.g., 99.9% uptime)
Allowed failure allowance before reliability improvements become mandatory
mleng: Centralized repository of computed features shared across models
Divergence between data at training time vs. inference time
Tracked ML run with fixed hyperparameters, data version, and metrics
Versioned catalog of trained models with metadata and deployment status
Reducing model precision (FP32→INT8) to shrink size and speed up inference
blockchain: Self-executing code deployed on-chain — immutable once deployed
Unit measuring computational cost of an Ethereum transaction
Application Binary Interface — schema for calling a smart contract
Decentralized Finance — financial protocols running on-chain without intermediaries
Zero-Knowledge Proof — proves a statement is true without revealing the input
science: Finite Element Method — numerical technique for solving PDEs on a mesh
High-Performance Computing — large-scale parallel computation on clusters
Ability to re-run a computation and get the exact same result
Workflow managers for reproducible scientific data pipelines
Full traceability of how a dataset or computed result was produced
immersive: Extended Reality — umbrella term covering VR, AR, and MR
Hierarchical data structure representing all objects in a 3D world
Audio system positioning sounds in 3D space relative to the listener
Virtual replica of a physical object updated with real-time sensor data
Browser API enabling VR/AR experiences without a native app install
integration: Entry point routing, rate-limiting, and authenticating all API requests
Storing state as an immutable sequence of events rather than current values
Command Query Responsibility Segregation — separate read and write models
Queue holding messages that failed processing after N retries
Test verifying consumer and provider agree on an API contract (Pact)
embedded: Real-Time OS — guarantees task execution within strict deterministic time bounds
Hardware Abstraction Layer — portable interface over device-specific hardware
Signal pausing the CPU to handle a high-priority hardware event immediately
Over-The-Air update — delivering signed firmware updates wirelessly
Hardware/software timer that resets the system if it stops responding
media: Converting media from one codec/format to another (e.g., H.264 → AV1)
HTTP Live Streaming — adaptive bitrate streaming protocol by Apple
Content Delivery Network — geographically distributed servers for low-latency delivery
Algorithm for encoding/decoding audio or video (H.264, VP9, AV1, AAC)
Set of quality levels (resolution + bitrate) for adaptive streaming
devtools: Language Server Protocol — standard for IDE features like autocomplete and go-to-def
Abstract Syntax Tree — parsed representation of source code for transformations
Automated script transforming source code according to a defined pattern
Semantic Versioning — MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH convention for release compatibility
Incremental parsing library for syntax highlighting and code analysis
fintech: Immutable accounting journal recording all financial transactions
Accounting method where every debit has a matching credit
Know Your Customer — identity verification required by financial regulation
Anti-Money Laundering — detecting and reporting suspicious financial activity
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard — mandatory for card data handling

contextBudget.base:
<!-- Always loaded — context economy and session hygiene -->

## Context Budget & Dreaming

### Rule 1 — One Chat Per Task
- ✅ One Claude session = one atomic, bounded task
- ✅ Before a long session: re-read `CLAUDE.local.md` for current state
- ❌ Do not chain multiple features without summarizing in between
- If > 15 exchanges in a session: summarize before continuing

### Rule 5 — CLAUDE.md Half-Life Reset
- Re-read `CLAUDE.md` every 10 active sessions
- Archive obsolete sections in `docs/decisions/`
- Goal: keep `CLAUDE.md` < 200 lines at all times
- Signal: if `CLAUDE.md` exceeds 250 lines → trigger a reset

### Rule 6 — Smart Ignoring
- Read `.claude/.claudeignore` before exploring the project
- Report hidden dependencies BEFORE touching a file
- Never recursively read `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `.venv/` without explicit reason

### Dreaming Protocol — End of session
Before closing a long session (> 30 min or > 15 exchanges):
1. Summarize decisions made in 3 lines → `CLAUDE.local.md`
2. Propose an ADR if an architectural decision was made
3. Check if `CLAUDE.md` needs updating
4. List pending tasks for the next session
contextBudget.expert_addon:
### Rule 2 — Dynamic Dependency Graph
- Read only files directly needed for the task
- Mentally build the dependency graph before acting
- Report context cost before exploring an entire module
- Prefer targeted analysis over global exploration

### Rule 7 — Memory Files Time-Decay
- `CLAUDE.local.md` files older than 7 days may be stale
- Verify freshness before applying session context
- Archive session contexts older than 30 days in `docs/sessions/`

### Rule 8 — Sub-agents Context Isolation
- Independent tasks → separate sub-agents (isolated context)
- Do not analyze an entire module in the main session
- Analysis results → output file, not inline in context
- Parallelize analyses that do not depend on each other

### Rule 9 — Metadata-First Indexing
- Start by reading `index.*`, `README.*`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.claude/rules/index.md`
- Build the project map before exploring source files
- Prefer reading the plan over discovering as you go

### Rule 3 — Prompt Caching (Managed Agents)
- Stable content (CLAUDE.md, rules) → at the top of the prompt to benefit from cache
- Dynamic instructions → at the bottom of the prompt
- Target ratio: 70% stable / 30% dynamic

pruningRules:
---
description: Refactoring and dead code removal — invoke explicitly with @.claude/rules/pruning.md
---

## Pruning / Refactoring Rules

### Before any deletion
1. Exhaustive `grep` to check direct AND dynamic usages
2. Check re-exports and barrel files (`index.ts`, `__init__.py`)
3. Check dynamic imports (`require()`, `importlib`, `reflect`)
4. Confirm with the user if there is any doubt

### Absolute constraints
- ❌ Never mix refactor and new feature in the same commit
- ❌ Never delete a file without exhaustive grep first
- ✅ Tests must pass BEFORE and AFTER each step
- ✅ Atomic commits: one refactor = one commit with message `refactor:`
- ✅ Immediate revert if tests break after deletion

### Protocol
1. Identify candidate code for removal
2. Check all references (direct + dynamic usage)
3. Remove in small, verifiable increments
4. Run tests after each deletion
5. Atomic commit with description of the change

### Before/after metrics
- Number of lines removed
- Number of files reduced
- Coverage maintained or improved

agentTemplates.code_reviewer:
---
name: code-reviewer
description: Expert code review. Reviews code for quality, security, and maintainability after writing.
tools: [Read, Grep, Glob, Bash]
model: {{model}}
---

You are a code review specialist for **{{project_name}}**.

When reviewing:
1. Check against `.claude/rules/quality-gates.md` metrics
2. Verify security rules from `.claude/rules/security.md`
3. Check language-specific rules in `.claude/rules/`
4. Verify test coverage if tests exist

Rate issues: CRITICAL (block) / HIGH (should fix) / MEDIUM (nice) / LOW (style).
Be specific: quote the code, explain the problem, show the fix.
agentTemplates.security:
---
name: security-reviewer
description: Security vulnerability detection before commits. Flags secrets, injection, unsafe crypto.
tools: [Read, Grep, Glob, Bash]
model: {{model}}
---

You are a security specialist for **{{project_name}}**.

Check for:
- Secrets / API keys in code or diffs
- SQL injection (string concatenation in queries)
- XSS (unsanitized user input in HTML)
- SSRF (user-controlled URLs in server requests)
- Insecure crypto (MD5, SHA1, weak keys)
- Missing authentication / authorization checks
- Sensitive data in logs or error messages
agentTemplates.tdd_guide:
---
name: tdd-guide
description: Enforces TDD workflow. Use for new features and bug fixes.
tools: [Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep]
model: {{model}}
---

You enforce TDD for **{{project_name}}**.

Workflow:
1. RED : Write the test. Run it. It MUST fail. If it passes, the test is wrong.
2. GREEN : Write minimum code to pass. Run again. Must pass.
3. VALIDATE : `{{lint_cmd}}` + full suite + coverage ≥ {{coverage}}%.

NEVER skip step 1. NEVER modify tests to make them pass.
agentTemplates.refactor:
---
name: refactor-cleaner
description: Safe dead code cleanup. Identifies and removes unused code carefully.
tools: [Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob]
model: {{model}}
---

You are a refactoring specialist for **{{project_name}}**.

1. Identify dead code (no references, unused imports)
2. Before removing: confirm no dynamic usage
3. Remove in small, verifiable increments
4. Tests must pass before AND after each step
5. Never mix refactoring with new features in the same commit
agentTemplates.build_resolver:
---
name: build-error-resolver
description: Build and type error resolution with minimal diffs.
tools: [Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob]
model: {{model}}
---

You fix build errors for **{{project_name}}** with minimal changes.

1. Read the full error message
2. Find the root cause (don't patch symptoms)
3. Make the smallest possible fix
4. Verify the build passes after
5. If architectural change needed, flag it — don't do it unilaterally

domainRules.apps:
## Domain: Software Apps & UI

### Architecture
- Separate UI layer from business logic — no business logic in components
- State: local state first, shared state only when multiple components need it
- API layer abstracted behind a typed client — never raw fetch() in components

### Constraints
- Accessibility: WCAG AA minimum — ARIA, keyboard navigation, contrast
- Performance: Core Web Vitals targets (LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1)
- Mobile-first responsive layout by default

### FORBIDDEN
- ❌ Business logic in UI components
- ❌ Direct API calls from components (use hooks / service layer)
- ❌ Interactive elements without keyboard accessibility

### Testing
- Unit: component-level with React Testing Library / Vue Test Utils
- Integration: user flows with MSW (Mock Service Worker)
- E2E: critical paths with Playwright
domainRules.data:
## Domain: Data Systems & Automation

### Architecture
- Medallion: Bronze (raw, immutable) → Silver (cleaned) → Gold (aggregated)
- Idempotent transforms: re-running must produce identical output
- Every dataset has a schema + data contract agreed with consumers

### Constraints
- Assert row counts before and after every transform
- Data quality gates block pipeline promotion (Great Expectations / dbt tests)
- Lineage tracked end-to-end — every column traces back to a source

### FORBIDDEN
- ❌ Mutate raw / Bronze tables
- ❌ Drop columns without downstream validation
- ❌ Silent row loss — missing records must trigger alerts

### Testing
- Unit: transform functions with fixed sample datasets
- Integration: end-to-end pipeline with test fixtures and row assertions
- Quality: dbt tests or Great Expectations on every model
domainRules.ai:
## Domain: AI Products & Knowledge Systems

### Architecture
- LLM calls wrapped in a typed service layer — no raw API calls in business logic
- Prompts versioned in code alongside their evals — no hardcoded strings in functions
- Eval harness must pass before any model or prompt change reaches production

### Constraints
- RAG: chunk 512 tokens, overlap 50 · hybrid search (BM25 + semantic) · cite sources
- Embeddings versioned with the model — reindex everything when model changes
- Token budget tracked and capped per call to control cost

### FORBIDDEN
- ❌ Deploy prompt changes without running the eval regression suite
- ❌ Send PII in prompts without anonymization
- ❌ Log raw LLM outputs containing user data

### Testing
- Unit: prompt templates, retrieval recall/precision on fixed queries
- Eval: regression suite on golden Q&A pairs (non-deterministic — use thresholds)
- Integration: end-to-end pipeline with known answers to validate full flow
domainRules.content:
## Domain: Content Creation & Communication

### Architecture
- Pillar + cluster: every cluster page links back to its pillar — no orphaned articles
- Semantic chunking (512 tokens, 50 overlap) for RAG-backed content pipelines
- Content pipeline: ingest → normalize → enrich → publish → monitor performance

### Constraints
- E-E-A-T: cite sources with dates · unique expertise angle · refresh cycle ≤ 18 months
- SEO: title < 60 chars · meta 120–155 chars · JSON-LD schema required on every page
- GEO/AEO: H2/H3 phrased as questions · clear definitions of domain terms

### FORBIDDEN
- ❌ Publish without metadata (title, meta, canonical, schema)
- ❌ Duplicate content across URLs without a canonical tag
- ❌ Images without descriptive, keyword-rich alt text

### Testing
- SEO audit: Lighthouse + Google Rich Results Test
- RAG: retrieval precision on sample queries before pipeline goes live
- Freshness: automated flag for articles older than 18 months
domainRules.business:
## Domain: Business Strategy & Org Tools

### Architecture
- Domain model first: entities and relationships defined before implementation
- Workflow engine for multi-step approval processes — no ad hoc state in code
- Audit log: immutable, append-only, queryable by user + date + action

### Constraints
- Role-based access control enforced at the service layer, not just the UI
- All destructive actions require explicit confirmation + audit log entry
- Report queries must not run on the primary transactional database

### FORBIDDEN
- ❌ Delete or mutate audit log entries — use correction entries
- ❌ Bypass role checks even for admin users in tests
- ❌ Run heavy analytics queries on the primary OLTP database

### Testing
- Unit: business rules and workflow state machine transitions
- Integration: role-based access scenarios across all permission combinations
- E2E: key approval flows with multiple actor roles
domainRules.security:
## Domain: Security, Quality & Reliability

### Architecture
- Security-as-code: threat models and policies in version control alongside source
- Defense in depth: multiple overlapping controls — no single point of failure
- SLO-driven reliability: define error budgets and alert thresholds before features

### Constraints
- OWASP Top 10 coverage required for every application component
- SAST on every commit · DAST on every staging deployment
- Incident response runbooks for every P1/P2 alert

### FORBIDDEN
- ❌ Merge without SAST passing
- ❌ Escalate privileges without an audit trail
- ❌ Skip threat modeling for new authentication or data access flows

### Testing
- SAST: Semgrep / Bandit / eslint-plugin-security
- DAST: OWASP ZAP on the staging environment
- Chaos: fault injection for resilience validation (k6, Gremlin, Chaos Monkey)
- Load: k6 / Locust with defined SLO pass/fail thresholds
domainRules.mleng:
## Domain: ML Engineering & Infrastructure

### Architecture
- Feature store decouples feature computation from model training
- Model registry is the single source of truth for all versioned models
- Serving layer is stateless — model weights loaded at startup, not per-request

### Constraints
- Track every experiment: code commit + dataset version + hyperparameters + metrics
- Same preprocessing code at training and inference — prevent training-serving skew
- Latency SLO defined (P99) and load-tested before every production rollout

### FORBIDDEN
- ❌ Deploy a model without a regression test on the evaluation set
- ❌ Use different preprocessing at training vs. serving time
- ❌ Commit model weights to git — use DVC, MLflow artifacts, or object storage

### Testing
- Unit: preprocessing transforms and feature engineering functions
- Eval: offline metrics on held-out test set compared to current production baseline
- Load: P99 latency and throughput at production-level concurrency
domainRules.blockchain:
## Domain: Decentralized & Blockchain

### Architecture
- Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern for all state-changing functions (reentrancy)
- Minimal on-chain logic: expensive computation belongs off-chain or Layer 2
- Events emitted for every significant state change (subgraph / indexer compatibility)

### Constraints
- Invariant fuzz testing (Foundry) required for all financial contract logic
- Gas optimization: measure before and after every change with snapshots
- Multi-sig governance for admin functions controlling significant value

### FORBIDDEN
- ❌ Deploy to mainnet without an independent third-party security audit
- ❌ Store private keys or seeds in code, config files, or environment variables
- ❌ Upgrade proxy patterns without timelock + multi-sig approval process

### Testing
- Unit: every function, full branch coverage (Foundry / Hardhat)
- Invariant: fuzz testing with Foundry stateful invariant tests
- Integration: forked mainnet tests for DeFi protocol interactions
- Formal: Certora or Halmos for critical financial math properties
domainRules.science:
## Domain: Scientific Computing & Modeling

### Architecture
- Reproducibility first: pin all software, data, and random seeds in workflow files
- Workflow DAG (Snakemake or Nextflow) for dependency tracking and parallelism
- Immutable raw data: never overwrite primary or reference datasets

### Constraints
- Numerical validation: compare against analytical solutions or known benchmarks
- HPC jobs: estimate memory and walltime before submission
- Reference datasets version-locked with hash verification

### FORBIDDEN
- ❌ Overwrite raw or primary data
- ❌ Run experiments without reproducibility metadata (seed, version, date)
- ❌ Skip numerical validation against a known reference implementation

### Testing
- Unit: numerical functions against reference or analytical implementations
- Regression: compare output to previously validated gold-standard runs
- Convergence: confirm solutions stabilize with finer grids or more iterations
domainRules.immersive:
## Domain: Immersive & Spatial Experiences

### Architecture
- Frame budget first: 90Hz VR requires < 11ms frame time — profile before adding features
- Scene hierarchy: group static objects · frustum cull aggressively · LOD for distance
- Input abstraction: device-agnostic layer that works on Quest, ARKit, and WebXR

### Constraints
- Comfort: no locomotion without user consent · minimum 72Hz for any VR experience
- Assets: compressed textures (KTX2/Basis) · GLTF for web · draw calls budgeted per frame
- Multiplayer: authoritative server for state · client-side prediction for responsiveness

### FORBIDDEN
- ❌ Frame drops below target Hz without a performance investigation and sign-off
- ❌ Skip motion sickness guidelines for VR locomotion (no artificial movement default)
- ❌ Release without testing on the actual weakest target device

### Testing
- Performance: GPU/CPU profiler on the weakest target device
- Comfort: motion sickness audit with real users for every VR locomotion type
- Network: multiplayer at 200ms simulated latency before release
domainRules.integration:
## Domain: Integration & Connectivity

### Architecture
- Idempotency keys on all API calls that trigger side effects
- Event-driven: producers publish facts, consumers react — eliminate temporal coupling
- Contract tests (Pact) required before any API change reaches dependent consumers

### Constraints
- Retry with exponential backoff + jitter on all outbound network calls
- Circuit breaker on every third-party dependency
- Dead letter queue for all async message processing with alerting on DLQ growth

### FORBIDDEN
- ❌ Synchronous call chains longer than 3 hops (latency compounds badly)
- ❌ Breaking API changes without a deprecation notice and migration period
- ❌ Process messages without idempotency guard (duplicate delivery is guaranteed)

### Testing
- Contract: Pact tests between every producer/consumer pair before deploy
- Integration: test against real message broker and real database, not mocks
- Chaos: broker downtime, network partition, and consumer lag simulation
domainRules.embedded:
## Domain: Embedded & Edge Computing

### Architecture
- HAL (Hardware Abstraction Layer): no direct register access in application code
- RTOS task priorities: hard real-time tasks isolated in dedicated high-priority tasks
- OTA updates: signed firmware + automatic rollback on integrity check failure

### Constraints
- No dynamic memory allocation in interrupt handlers or hard-real-time tasks
- Stack sizes profiled and validated under worst-case conditions before production
- Watchdog timer always active in production — never disable for debugging

### FORBIDDEN
- ❌ Blocking calls inside ISRs — use notification flags or message queues
- ❌ Deploy firmware without cryptographic signing and integrity verification
- ❌ Remove the watchdog timer for any reason (use a longer timeout to debug)

### Testing
- Unit: business logic via HAL mock layer on the host (fast iteration)
- Hardware-in-the-loop: all timing-critical paths on the real target hardware
- Stress: 72h burn-in under maximum load for production embedded systems
domainRules.media:
## Domain: Media & Streaming Systems

### Architecture
- Pipeline stages scale independently: ingest · transcode · package · CDN deliver
- Adaptive bitrate: minimum 3 quality levels · 2–6s segments · HLS + DASH outputs
- CDN-first: origin server must never receive direct user traffic in production

### Constraints
- Startup SLO: < 3s first frame for live · < 1s for on-demand at median connection
- Codec selection: H.264 (compatibility) + AV1/HEVC (bandwidth) where device supports
- GPU transcoding: CPU fallback path must exist and be tested for all pipelines

### FORBIDDEN
- ❌ Re-encode unnecessarily — remux when only the container format changes
- ❌ Single ingest point without failover (single point of failure for live)
- ❌ Store master originals in a lossy codec format

### Testing
- Quality: VMAF/SSIM scores vs. original at every bitrate ladder level
- Load: concurrent stream test at 2× expected peak concurrency
- Failover: CDN origin failover and ingest redundancy tested in staging
domainRules.devtools:
## Domain: Developer Tools & Infrastructure

### Architecture
- Plugin architecture: stable core API · extensions are isolated and independently versioned
- LSP / DAP protocol: implement spec correctly · test against multiple editor clients
- CLI: POSIX conventions · exit codes (0 success, 1 error, 2 misuse) · --json for scripting

### Constraints
- Backward compatibility: semver strictly followed · breaking changes require MAJOR bump
- Performance: CLI startup < 200ms · LSP response < 100ms at P95
- Cross-platform: CI matrix covering Linux, macOS, and Windows for every release

### FORBIDDEN
- ❌ Break CLI flags or public API without a deprecation notice in a prior MINOR release
- ❌ Publish to npm/PyPI/crates without a changelog entry and semver bump
- ❌ Release without the full cross-platform CI matrix passing

### Testing
- Unit: parsing, AST transforms, command dispatch logic
- Integration: test against real project fixtures, not minimal toy examples
- Compatibility: verify against the minimum supported editor / runtime versions
domainRules.fintech:
## Domain: Financial & Regulated Transactions

### Architecture
- Double-entry ledger: every debit has a matching credit — balances computed, never stored
- Immutable audit trail: correction entries only — no updates or deletes ever
- Payment processing isolated from business logic in a dedicated PCI-scoped module

### Constraints
- PCI-DSS: card data handled only in certified scope — tokenize at the boundary immediately
- KYC/AML: identity verification required before transactions above regulatory threshold
- Idempotency keys required on every payment API call (duplicate requests must be safe)
- Money amounts: always Decimal/integer cents — never IEEE 754 floats

### FORBIDDEN
- ❌ Log raw card numbers, CVV, or full PANs — even in debug or test mode
- ❌ Mutate ledger entries — use reversal/correction entries
- ❌ Store card data outside a PCI-DSS certified storage scope
- ❌ Deploy payment flows without end-to-end transaction replay tests

### Testing
- Unit: ledger math with Decimal precision · rounding rules · currency edge cases
- Integration: full payment lifecycle with Stripe test mode / provider sandbox
- Security: PCI scope penetration test before go-live
- Compliance: regulatory edge cases (refunds, chargebacks, disputes, FX)

rag.libs: LangChain, LlamaIndex, Chroma, Weaviate, Pinecone, pgvector
rag.pattern: Chunk 512 tokens with 50-token overlap · hybrid search (BM25 + semantic) · always cite sources in output
rag.pitfall: Version embeddings together with the model — changing the model requires a full reindex

agents.libs: LangChain, LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, smolagents, MCP
agents.pattern: Explicit termination conditions on every agent loop · human-in-the-loop checkpoints for destructive actions
agents.pitfall: Unbounded loops and runaway tool calls — always set max_iterations and a cost/token budget cap

ai_eval.libs: MLflow, W&B, Promptfoo, LangSmith, RAGAS, DeepEval
ai_eval.pattern: Golden dataset of Q&A pairs version-locked in the repo · eval runs as a CI gate on every prompt change
ai_eval.pitfall: Non-determinism — use temperature=0 for regression evals and score thresholds rather than exact match

payments.pattern: Idempotency keys on every payment call · webhook signature verification required · Decimal for all amounts
payments.pitfall: FORBIDDEN: log raw card numbers or CVV even in debug mode — tokenize at point of entry

banking_core.libs: Plaid, Open Banking APIs, IBAN validator, Moov
banking_core.pattern: Double-entry ledger — every debit has a matching credit · PSD2 SCA for strong customer authentication
banking_core.pitfall: FORBIDDEN: mutable ledger rows — use reversal entries; never UPDATE a posted transaction

fraud.libs: Stripe Radar, Sift, custom ML scoring pipeline
fraud.pattern: Real-time scoring at transaction time (< 100ms) · velocity checks per card + IP + device fingerprint
fraud.pitfall: False positive rate matters as much as recall — tune thresholds with business stakeholders

smart_contracts.pattern: Checks-Effects-Interactions for reentrancy · invariant fuzz testing · emit events for every state change
smart_contracts.pitfall: FORBIDDEN: deploy to mainnet without an independent third-party security audit

training.pattern: Track every run: code commit + dataset hash + seed + hyperparameters · reproducible with fixed seed
training.pitfall: Training-serving skew — apply identical preprocessing to validation and serving data

deployment.libs: BentoML, TorchServe, ONNX Runtime, Triton Inference Server
deployment.pattern: Quantize before serving · shadow traffic before full rollout · A/B compare vs. production baseline
deployment.pitfall: FORBIDDEN: skip P99 latency SLO validation before routing production traffic to a new model

experiments.pattern: Every experiment logged automatically · compare runs in a UI before promoting · artifacts stored in object store
experiments.pitfall: Comparing experiments trained on different dataset splits — always lock the eval split

pentest.libs: Burp Suite, Nmap, OWASP ZAP, Metasploit, SQLMap
pentest.pattern: OWASP Top 10 coverage · document findings with CVSS score · reproduce in staging before reporting
pentest.pitfall: FORBIDDEN: run any scan or exploit without explicit written authorization from the target owner

observability.libs: Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, Jaeger, PagerDuty, Loki
observability.pattern: Three pillars: metrics (RED) + traces (distributed) + logs (structured JSON) · alert on SLO burn rate
observability.pitfall: Alert fatigue — only page on SLO violations, not on symptoms; tune thresholds before going live

etl.libs: dbt, Apache Spark, Flink, Beam, Airbyte
etl.pattern: Idempotent transforms — re-running produces identical output · assert row counts at each stage boundary
etl.pitfall: Silent data loss — always validate that source row count matches target after every transform

api_gw.libs: Kong, AWS API Gateway, Nginx, Envoy, tRPC, FastAPI
api_gw.pattern: OpenAPI spec first · rate limiting and auth at the gateway layer · semver for all breaking changes
api_gw.pitfall: FORBIDDEN: break the existing response schema without a deprecation period and consumer migration

event_driven.libs: Apache Kafka, RabbitMQ, AWS SQS/SNS, NATS, Temporal
event_driven.pattern: Idempotency guard on every consumer · dead letter queue with alerting · at-least-once delivery assumed
event_driven.pitfall: Ordering guarantees are partition-level only in Kafka — design consumers to handle out-of-order events

iot.libs: MQTT (Mosquitto, AWS IoT Core), Zephyr RTOS, ESP-IDF, Eclipse Hono
iot.pattern: Store-and-forward for intermittent connectivity · TLS mutual auth · firmware signing for OTA
iot.pitfall: FORBIDDEN: clear-text MQTT on production devices — even on internal networks

firmware.libs: FreeRTOS, Zephyr, STM32 HAL, ESP-IDF, libopencm3
firmware.pattern: No dynamic allocation in ISRs or hard-RT tasks · watchdog on all production builds · HAL for portability
firmware.pitfall: FORBIDDEN: blocking calls inside interrupt service routines — use notification flags or queues

video_image.libs: FFmpeg, OpenCV, GStreamer, PyAV, Pillow, libvips
video_image.pattern: GPU-accelerated transcoding with CPU fallback · validate codec support before pipeline build · batch process
video_image.pitfall: Re-encoding unnecessarily — prefer remux (copy codec) over transcode when only the container changes

live_stream.libs: WebRTC (mediasoup, Janus), HLS.js, Nginx-RTMP, AWS IVS, Wowza
live_stream.pattern: Adaptive bitrate with 3+ quality levels · startup SLO < 3s · CDN origin failover tested in staging
live_stream.pitfall: Single ingest point in production — always provision redundant ingest for live streams

cli.libs: oclif, cobra, typer, click, clap (Rust), arg
cli.pattern: POSIX exit codes (0/1/2) · --json flag for machine-readable output · consistent --help format
cli.pitfall: Breaking CLI flag changes without a deprecation cycle — semver applies to CLI interfaces too

secure_code.pattern: SAST in CI on every commit · threat model before implementing auth or data flows · OWASP Top 10 checklist
secure_code.pitfall: Security as a last step — integrate scanning into CI from day one, not at the pre-release audit

bioinformatics.libs: Nextflow, Snakemake, BioPython, GATK, Bioconda, Biopandas
bioinformatics.pattern: Containerize every tool version · version-lock reference genomes with hash · Nextflow/Snakemake DAGs
bioinformatics.pitfall: Non-reproducibility — always pin tool versions and reference database versions explicitly in workflow files
